economic_notes: GBEs operate in energy (Twin Falls hydro, Umbata Falls hydro), mining labour services (Biiwaabikoke LP), contracting in forestry/mining/energy/construction (BMI-Maamwigaaboo), and commercial real estate (Wiigiiwaaganag LP). Capacity funding received from Williams Operating Corporation for economic development. Road maintenance services provided to Nawiingnokiima Forest Management Corporation.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-compliance with PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations — management has not completed its assessment of tangible capital assets for potential AROs; impact cannot be quantified. Consolidated entities include Biigtigong Dbenjgan Ltd., Begetekong Trust, and MKWA Contracting GP Ltd. Five GBEs/GBPs accounted for under modified equity method: Twin Falls LP (97% owned, hydro), Umbata Falls LP (51% owned, hydro), Biiwaabikoke LP (100% owned, mining labour), BMI-Maamwigaaboo Contracting LP (48% owned, forestry/mining/construction), Wiigiiwaaganag LP (48% owned, commercial real estate). Large increase in deferred revenue from $17.1M to $46.6M driven primarily by Water and Sewer ISC funding of $32.3M received. Significant land claims (Aboriginal Rights and Title) against Crown in Canada and Ontario recorded as contingent asset. GBE fiscal year ends are December 31, creating a three-month lag. Total government transfers calculated as federal ($24,583,607) plus provincial ($3,140,647) = $27,724,254; OSR = $41,906,917 - $27,724,254 = $14,182,663 (includes KEB, OFNLP, capacity funding, rent, other, and GBE earnings).
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
